title: STANDARD FOR PASSION FRUIT CODEX STAN 316-2014

sections.section: STANDARD FOR PASSION FRUIT CODEX STAN 316-2014
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Passion Fruit (Codex Stan 316-2014); not a substantive provision.

Defines scope: applies to commercial varieties of passion fruit (Passiflora ligularis, Passiflora edulis forma edulis, Passiflora edulis forma flavicarpa and their hybrids) supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging; excludes passion fruit for industrial processing.

Sets quality provisions for passion fruit, specifying minimum quality attributes (e.g., whole, fresh‑appearing, firm, sound, clean, practically pest‑free, correct ripeness to withstand transport) and classifies fruit into three quality classes ('Extra', Class I, Class II) with differing allowable superficial defects.

Specifies minimum requirements for all classes: fruit must be whole, fresh in appearance, firm, sound (no rot), clean, practically free of pests, free of abnormal external moisture/foreign odour/cracking, with stem/stalk present (specific for golden passion fruit), appropriately ripe and capable of withstanding transport to destination.

Classifies passion fruit into three classes—'Extra', Class I and Class II—describing 'Extra' as superior with virtually no defects, Class I allowing slight superficial defects (e.g., up to 10% skin scratches) and Class II permitting more pronounced defects (e.g., up to 20% skin defects) provided the flesh is unaffected.

'Extra' class: fruit of superior quality, characteristic of the variety and essentially free of defects except very slight superficial ones that do not affect appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

Class I: fruit of good quality, characteristic of the variety, permitting slight shape defects, slight skin scratches (not exceeding 10% of the total surface) and slight colour defects provided these do not affect the flesh, quality, keeping quality or presentation; maturity may be gauged visually and confirmed by soluble solids and titratable acidity.

Class II: includes fruit that meet the minimum requirements but not higher classes, allowing defects in shape (including stalk extension), skin scratches or roughness not exceeding 20% of the surface and colour defects, provided the flesh is unaffected and essential quality and keeping characteristics remain.

Allows sizing by count, diameter or weight (or existing trading practice) with package labelling required and provides optional guidance tables for diameter size codes (e.g., A >78 mm) and weight size codes (e.g., A >139 g).

Specifies tolerances: quality tolerances by class (Extra up to 5% by number/weight not meeting 'Extra' but meeting Class I; Class I up to 10% not meeting Class I but meeting Class II; Class II up to 10% failing class/minimum except rotten/unfit fruit) and size tolerances of 10% by number/weight for fruit one size above or below the indicated size.

Details quality tolerances for each class: 'Extra' 5% by number/weight (allowed to meet Class I), Class I 10% (allowed to meet Class II), and Class II 10% (allowed even if not meeting class or minimum requirements, excluding rotten/unfit produce).
'Extra' class tolerance: permits up to 5% by number or weight of passion fruits not satisfying 'Extra' requirements but meeting Class I or within its tolerances.
Class I tolerance: permits up to 10% by number or weight of passion fruits not satisfying Class I requirements but meeting Class II or within its tolerances.

Class II tolerance: permits up to 10% by number or weight of passion fruits that satisfy neither the class nor the minimum requirements, excluding produce affected by rotting or otherwise unfit for consumption.
Size tolerances: for all classes, permits 10% by number or weight of fruit corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package.

Sets presentation requirements: packages must be uniform (same origin, variety, quality, colour and size) with contents representative; fruit must be packed to protect them using new, clean, non‑toxic materials, comply with CAC/RCP 44‑1995, and containers must meet hygiene, ventilation and resistance standards and be free of foreign matter and smell.
Requires each package to contain only passion fruits uniform in origin, variety, quality, colour and size, and that the visible portion be representative of the entire contents.

Requires passion fruits to be packed to protect the produce using new, clean, non‑damaging materials (printing/labels allowed if non‑toxic) and mandates packing in accordance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44‑1995).

Requires containers to meet quality, hygiene, ventilation and strength characteristics to ensure proper handling, shipping and preservation of passion fruits, and that packages be free of foreign matter and odours.

Sets labelling requirements for consumer and non‑retail packages, covering nature of produce, identification of exporter/packer/dispatcher, origin, commercial identification (class, size, optional net weight), and optional official inspection mark, and references the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1‑1985).
States that consumer packages must comply with CODEX STAN 1‑1985 labelling requirements and the additional specific provisions in this standard.
Requires non‑visible consumer packages to be labelled with the name of the produce (golden/sweet granadilla, purple passion fruit, or yellow passion fruit) and permits optional indication of variety.
Requires non‑retail packages to bear specified particulars legibly and indelibly on the same side, visible from the outside or provided in accompanying shipment documents.
Requires name and address of the exporter, packer and/or dispatcher to be shown on non‑retail containers and allows an optional identification code.
Requires non‑retail containers to state the name of the produce (golden/sweet granadilla, purple passion fruit, yellow passion fruit) if contents are not visible, with variety name optional.
Requires declaration of country of origin and optionally the district, national, regional or local place name on non‑retail containers.
Requires commercial identification on non‑retail containers including class, size (by count, size code and range, or size range) and optional net weight.
States that an official inspection mark may be applied to the package (optional).
Requires compliance with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CODEX STAN 193‑1995) and with Codex pesticide maximum residue limits for the produce covered by this standard.
Recommends that handling and preparation follow Codex hygiene texts (CAC/RCP 1‑1969, CAC/RCP 53‑2003) and that the produce comply with any microbiological criteria established under CAC/GL 21‑1997.
